summary: Split fighter library code into a new matched C translation unit, src/melee/ft/ftlib.c, with public prototypes in src/melee/ft/ftlib.h. The PR also performed treewide relabeling using names exposed by ftlib assert strings and introduced src/melee/it/id.h for item kind constants, replacing raw or local item IDs in fighter, stage, player, and asm references. Review focused on preserving struct offsets in field names and using internal item terminology such as AfterImage rather than Shadow.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/ftlib.c with 987 lines of matched C for fighter-library routines around the 0x800860C4-0x800877D4 range, including fighter iteration, target selection, position/accessor helpers, collision helpers, and status/query style functions.
Added src/melee/ft/ftlib.h with prototypes for the newly split ftlib functions and included fighter.h plus HSD gobj declarations.

Moved a large region out of asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s; the file's section range was narrowed to 0x8008521C-0x80086194, while asm/melee/ft/code_800877F8.s and asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s were added to keep remaining assembly/data split correctly.
Updated build lists in obj_files.mk and e_files.mk so the new ftlib C object and split asm/data layout participate in the build.

Renamed Fighter.xE0_airState to Fighter.xE0_ground_or_air and added GA_Ground and GA_Air enum values in fighter.h, then updated C users to compare against GA_Ground or GA_Air instead of raw 0/1 where shown.
Added src/melee/it/id.h containing an It_Kind_* enum based on the SSBM datasheet and used it in places such as grstory.c, replacing a local ITEM_SHYGUY define with It_Kind_Heiho.

Relabeled calls to func_8026B300 as itGetKind across many assembly files, including ftfalcon.s, ftlink.s, ftpeach.s, code_8007C114.s, grcastle.s, grkinokoroute.s, grkongo.s, grlib.s, code_80266F3C.s, mpcoll.s, and mplib.s.
Applied smaller include/prototype and naming cleanups across fighter character source files, player files, and stage files to align with the new headers and item/fighter naming.
smart_moves: Used assert-string evidence from ftlib to justify treewide HAL-style names instead of treating the matched file as an isolated C drop.
Created a dedicated ftlib.h instead of leaving the new matched functions only in a global catch-all header, making future fighter-library work easier to include and search.
Introduced an item-kind header before performing broad item relabeling, enabling shared constants such as It_Kind_Heiho and It_Kind_FalcoAfterimage to replace local defines or numeric IDs.
Preserved offset information in the fighter field name xE0_ground_or_air, satisfying review guidance while still making the semantic meaning clear.
Kept unmatched/split assembly organized by address range rather than leaving the old huge code_8008521C.s monolith intact.
Replaced raw air/ground integer checks with GA_Ground and GA_Air where evidence supported the enum, improving readability without changing layout.
decomp_lessons: When a matched module exposes internal names through assert strings, use those strings to guide treewide relabeling, but keep offset prefixes on struct fields until the project convention allows dropping them.
For large asm-to-C splits, update the assembly section ranges and build object lists in the same PR so address ownership remains clear.
A new semantic enum is safest when it is tied to an existing struct field or known ID list; GA_Ground/GA_Air documents Fighter.xE0_ground_or_air without changing its storage type.
Shared item IDs should live in a central header instead of per-file defines; this avoids inconsistent names for the same item kind.
Reviewers may prefer internal engine terminology over ambiguous gameplay descriptions; in this PR, AfterImage was preferred over Shadow because Shadow could mean a floor shadow.
Field names in decomp code often need both semantic meaning and layout evidence, e.g. xE0_ground_or_air rather than just ground_or_air.
naming_conventions: Fighter struct fields retained offset prefixes: xE0_ground_or_air was preferred over ground_or_air.
Ground/air state enum values used GA_Ground = 0 and GA_Air = 1.
Item kind constants used the It_Kind_* prefix in src/melee/it/id.h.
Internal item names were preferred where known: It_Kind_FalcoAfterimage rather than It_Kind_FalcoShadow.
The function func_8026B300 was relabeled to itGetKind, and its callers in both C and assembly were updated.
Character/item comments in id.h kept common English names alongside internal or Japanese-style labels when evidence was available, such as It_Kind_Taru with comment Barrel.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split a large contiguous assembly file by narrowing asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s to its remaining address range and adding asm/melee/ft/code_800877F8.s for later code.
Moved matched functions into a new C file while retaining unresolved labels and data in separate assembly/data files.
Performed symbol-only relabeling in assembly call sites, such as changing bl func_8026B300 to bl itGetKind, without altering instructions.
Used explicit unused local arrays and temporaries in ftlib.c, such as u32 unused[2] and u32 unused[4], indicating stack-shape preservation for matching.
Kept function names as address-based func_80086xxx where semantic names were not yet established, avoiding unsupported renames.

review_feedback.comment: I'd still like the naming to retain the offset.
review_feedback.applied_lesson: Do not drop offset prefixes prematurely on struct fields; use names like xE0_ground_or_air.

review_feedback.comment: Call it AfterImage. That's the internal name in structs and makes more sense than Shadow which would be their floor shadow, if you didn't know better.
review_feedback.applied_lesson: Prefer known internal terminology for item/effect names; avoid ambiguous gameplay terms.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftlib func_80086xxx functions to semantic names.
Find later uses or expansions of src/melee/it/id.h item kind constants.
Check whether xE0_ground_or_air remains the project convention or is later renamed after struct stabilization.
Search for additional HAL assert-string based names in fighter modules.
Find PRs that continue splitting asm/melee/ft/code_800877F8.s or ftdata.s.
